title: Configuration Registry And History Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the configuration registry and history capability, recovered from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage, not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facet descriptions are concise source-backed summaries citing verified manifest rows; proof is hermetic/stale, not current readiness. Enrich facet detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: A configuration definition moves through active, deprecated, and internal states, and the runtime resolves an effective value per tenant from either the platform default or a tenant override. Setting an override performs an audit-first upsert and clearing it performs an audit-first delete, after which a best-effort change-notification event is emitted; the audit precedes the persistence mutation so the change is always recorded.

stateModel.description: State is split between a pure, serialisable registry of configuration definitions (each carrying storage key, value type such as string/enum/boolean/json, allowed values, tenant-overridability, lifecycle, and per-definition read/write permissions) and the persisted tenant override values. The effective value carries a source marker distinguishing platform default from tenant override, and feature-flag compatibility transforms are applied during resolution.

permissions.description: Reads require a tenant-scoped configuration-read permission and additionally filter the visible definitions by each definition's required read permission; mutations require a tenant-scoped configuration-write permission and the use case re-enforces each definition's required write permission. Feature-flag definitions require dedicated feature permissions, and all resolution is tenant-isolated so one tenant never observes another tenant's overrides.

contracts.description: The interface exposes a list operation filterable by category that returns serialised definitions paired with effective value and source, a patch operation that sets a single key's override from a supplied value, and a delete operation that clears an override. Operation semantics are mediated by a configuration-store port with a persistent adapter so the contract is independent of the storage technology.

validation.description: Validation rejects unknown keys, non-overridable definitions, missing values, enum values outside the declared allowed set, and type mismatches against the definition's value type, and it honours category filters and per-definition permission scope. These invariants are enforced in the use case rather than only at the edge, so the same rules hold for every caller.

errorModel.description: Outcomes are deterministic and fail-closed: invalid input maps to a validation error, unknown keys to not-found, non-overridable definitions to an unprocessable-entity outcome, insufficient permission to forbidden, and absence of tenant context to a bad-request; successful writes and deletes return no-content. Best-effort change-notification fan-out is decoupled and its failure never rolls back the committed configuration mutation.

auditModel.description: A set operation emits the definition's configured change audit action and a clear operation emits a value-cleared audit action, in both cases before the tenant settings are mutated and carrying key and value metadata. Audit emission is a precondition for the write, so a failed audit prevents the persistence change entirely.

readinessModel.description: The capability is invariant-ready once the tenant settings store is reachable; the definition registry itself is pure and serialisable and therefore always available, while the history/audit projection of past changes is validated separately. When the persistence substrate is unavailable the capability degrades explicitly rather than silently serving stale or partial values.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by unit proofs over the configuration use case and contract validation (definition resolution, override source, validation rules, and audit-first writes) and by an adjacent live history projection proof that validates the audit/history record of configuration changes against a real substrate. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
